aliases: Southern California, Latin Playboys Car Club
description: American street gang founded in Los Angeles County, California in the mid-1950s, originally a car club. It has multiple cliques and is predominantly Hispanic, with an estimated 6,500 members. The gang is aligned with the Mexican Mafia (La EME) and engages in drug trafficking, assault, robbery, and other criminal activities. It remains active across several U.S. states and Mexico.

country_links.evidence_quote: Territory: Los Angeles County Washington, Utah, Oregon, Pennsylvania , Mexico, & Texas
country_links.context: The Playboys gang has territory in Mexico.
